title: Healthcare
description: Hospitals, pharmacies, insurance and finding English-speaking care in Belgium.

items.title: Health Insurance
items.description: Belgium has mandatory health insurance for residents through a 'mutualité'/'ziekenfonds'. Visitors should carry travel medical insurance covering at least €30,000.
steps: Register with a recognised mutualité/ziekenfonds within your first months of residence
Get a Belgian national registry number first (via municipality registration)
Keep Schengen-compliant travel insurance for short visits
tips: Employers often help arrange mutualité enrolment
EU nationals can use an EHIC; non-EU visitors need private travel insurance

items.title: Finding a GP (huisarts/médecin généraliste)
items.description: Registering with a local GP gives you a consistent point of contact and referrals to specialists.
steps: Ask your commune, employer or university for a recommended local GP list
Book an initial appointment to register as a patient
Keep your national registry / insurance details on hand
tips: Many GPs in Brussels speak English
Non-urgent same-day appointments can be hard to get — book ahead

items.title: Hospitals
items.description: Belgium has a high standard of hospital care. Major university hospitals with international patient services include Cliniques universitaires Saint-Luc, H.U.B (Hôpital Universitaire de Bruxelles), CHIREC and Erasmus Hospital in Brussels, and Antwerp University Hospital (UZA) in Antwerp.
tips: Cliniques universitaires Saint-Luc treats patients in French and English, among other languages
H.U.B International Patient Services supports patients from 70+ countries with multilingual staff
UZA (Drie Eikenstraat 655, 2650 Edegem, near Antwerp — tel. +32 3 821 30 00) offers interpreter services on request

items.title: Pharmacies
items.description: Pharmacies (apotheek/pharmacie) are widely available and marked with a green cross.
tips: A rotating on-duty pharmacy operates outside normal hours — look for the notice posted on any pharmacy door or check pharmacie.be

items.title: English-Speaking Clinics
items.description: Several clinics in Brussels cater specifically to the international and expat community.
tips: Berlaymont Health Centre (Brussels, tel. 02 735 53 26) offers English-language consultations, paediatrics, gynaecology, mental health and more under one roof
H.U.B International Patient Services provides dedicated support (translators, travel/visa assistance) for international patients

meta.Brussels: Berlaymont Health Centre, H.U.B International Patient Services
